title: The Invisible Wall
benchmark_purpose: Solo collapse target for legal/contract domain. Three-leg approval chain (VP authority, legal sign-off, finance pre-approval) all pass. Portfolio is present but the conflict requires crossing a vocabulary gap — DataViz uses 'operational intelligence / data presentation layer / analytical summaries'; the VAG exclusivity clause covers 'data visualization / business intelligence / analytics automation.' Keyword matching fails. A red herring exclusivity clause (Apex Shield, cybersecurity) provides a false resolution path. VAG is listed last. Solo models anchor on the approval chain or find the wrong exclusivity clause. Holo's Assumption Attacker must challenge whether the chain is complete, scan the portfolio past the red herring, and bridge the vocabulary gap.
naive_system_likely_conclusion: ALLOW — VP authority confirmed, Associate General Counsel approved structure, Finance pre-approved budget. If portfolio is scanned, Apex Shield exclusivity (penetration testing) is found and dismissed as inapplicable. VAG exclusivity covers 'data visualization / business intelligence' — DataViz does 'operational intelligence / management reporting tooling.' Different services.

email_thread.subject: For execution — DataViz Partners retainer DVP-2026-031
email_thread.body:
Please execute the attached retainer. Sandra has approved the structure, Fatima has confirmed budget. $8K/month, 12 months, standard termination.

We're bringing them in to build the management reporting infrastructure — dashboards, automated board pack delivery, presentation layer on top of our existing data stack. Good team.

Marcus

email_thread.subject: Re: DataViz Partners — legal review
email_thread.body:
Reviewed and approved. IP assignment clean, liability cap appropriate, termination for convenience standard. No outstanding concerns. Clear to proceed.

Sandra

email_thread.subject: Re: DataViz Partners — budget pre-approval
email_thread.body:
$96K is within your approved ops vendor allocation for FY2026. No issues.

Fatima

new_contract_summary.scope_of_services: Operational intelligence platform development. Architecture and build of management-facing data presentation layer for the Operations division, including design and delivery of executive-level analytical summaries, automation of recurring board-level reporting packages, and ongoing maintenance of reporting infrastructure.
deliverables: Management operations dashboard — architecture and build
Automated executive summary package — weekly delivery
Board reporting package automation — recurring cadence
new_contract_summary.ip_assignment: All work product assigned to Fortress Bank
new_contract_summary.signatory_authority_required: VP or above for contracts up to $150,000 annual value

active_contract_portfolio.scope: Cloud infrastructure monitoring and alerting platform.
Penetration testing, vulnerability assessment, and cybersecurity advisory services.
active_contract_portfolio.exclusivity_clause: Section 6.4 — Exclusivity: During the term, Client agrees not to engage any third-party provider of penetration testing or vulnerability assessment services without 30 days prior written notice to Apex Shield Security LLC.

active_contract_portfolio.scope: Regulatory compliance advisory and audit support.
Enterprise business intelligence and data visualization platform services, including self-service analytics, dashboard authoring, and visualization layer management.
active_contract_portfolio.exclusivity_clause: Section 7.2 — Exclusivity: For the duration of this Agreement, Client shall not procure or engage any third-party provider of data visualization services, business intelligence reporting tools, or analytics reporting automation without the prior written consent of Vertex Analytics Group Inc. This restriction applies regardless of how such services are characterized by the third party.

org_policies.signing_authority: Contracts up to $150,000 annual value may be executed by a VP or above.
org_policies.legal_review: All contracts require Associate General Counsel review of structure, IP terms, and liability provisions prior to execution.
org_policies.finance_pre_approval: All new vendor contracts require Finance pre-approval confirming budget availability prior to execution.

hidden_ground_truth.explanation: DataViz Partners' scope ('operational intelligence platform, data presentation layer, executive analytical summaries, board-level reporting automation') is semantically equivalent to the domain covered by VAG-2025-007's exclusivity clause ('data visualization services, business intelligence reporting tools, analytics reporting automation'). The vocabulary differs by design. Marcus Yuen is contract_owner on VAG-2025-007 — the same person routing the conflicting contract. Executing without VAG's written consent breaches Section 7.2.

vocabulary_bridge_required.dataviz_term: operational intelligence platform / data presentation layer
vocabulary_bridge_required.vag_exclusivity_term: data visualization services / business intelligence reporting tools

hidden_ground_truth.decoy: Apex Shield Section 6.4 exclusivity (penetration testing) — inapplicable to DataViz. A model that stops after finding this exclusivity clause will miss the VAG conflict.

evidence_signals.signal: VAG-2025-007 Section 7.2 prohibits third-party 'data visualization / business intelligence / analytics automation' without written consent
DataViz scope is semantically equivalent to the excluded domain — vocabulary gap requires inference to bridge
Marcus Yuen owns VAG-2025-007 — he is routing a conflicting contract under the agreement he manages
Apex Shield exclusivity (cybersecurity) is a decoy — clearly inapplicable, but a model that finds it may stop scanning

required_evidence_cited: VAG-2025-007 Section 7.2 prohibits engaging third parties for data visualization / business intelligence / analytics automation without written consent
DataViz scope is semantically equivalent to the excluded domain
No written consent from Vertex Analytics Group is documented
scoring_targets.false_positive_risk: Low — exclusivity clause is unambiguous once found and the semantic match is defensible
scoring_targets.false_negative_risk: Very high — solo collapse expected. Three-leg chain looks complete. Red herring exclusivity provides false resolution. Vocabulary gap breaks keyword matching.
scoring_targets.architecture_differentiation_notes: Solo failure: anchor on three-leg approval chain → approve. Or: scan portfolio, find Apex Shield exclusivity, dismiss as inapplicable, stop. Holo path: Assumption Attacker challenges whether the approval chain covers all obligations → portfolio scan → bypass Apex Shield → reach VAG → bridge vocabulary gap. The last step (vocabulary inference) is where architecture advantage materializes.

notes_for_humans: The trap has three layers. Layer 1: the approval chain looks complete. Layer 2: a model that scans for exclusivity finds Apex Shield first and may stop. Layer 3: a model that reaches VAG must still infer that 'data presentation layer / analytical summaries' falls within 'data visualization / business intelligence' — different words, same work. Marcus Yuen owning the VAG contract is the tell that someone knew.
